title: Syntax-Infused Transformer and BERT models for Machine Translation and Natural Language Understanding

review_output.candidate_title: Syntax-Infused Transformer and BERT models for Machine Translation and Natural Language Understanding

source_record_provenance.record_title: Syntax-Infused Transformer and BERT models for Machine Translation and Natural Language Understanding

satisfied_inclusion_points: 研究以 Transformer-based 模型 (Transformer, BERT) 為主題
對 TLMs 中的語法資訊進行了實驗性評估與分析（BLEU/GLUE 結果與 attention 可視化）
evidence_highlights: Abstract: "We show that the syntax-infused Transformer ... improvement of 0.7 BLEU ... and a maximum improvement of 1.99 BLEU points ..."
Abstract: "the incorporation of syntax into BERT fine-tuning outperforms baseline on a number of downstream tasks from the GLUE benchmark."
Full text: "We suggest a modification to the embeddings fed into the Transformer... incorporate POS, case, and subword position" (method describes syntax features added to Transformer and BERT).
Full text, Experiments/Table 1: reports BLEU improvements on WMT '14 EN-DE comparing baseline vs syntax-infused Transformer (quantitative empirical evaluation).
Full text, Experiments/Table 2: reports GLUE test results where BERTBASE + POS outperforms BERTBASE on multiple tasks (empirical evaluation).
Full text: "Examining the attention weights learned by the proposed model further justifies the effectiveness of incorporating syntactic features." (analysis of attention behavior)
review_output.decision_rationale: The paper explicitly studies Transformer-based models (Transformer encoder-decoder and BERT) and modifies their embeddings to inject syntactic features (POS, case, subword tags). It presents empirical evaluations: BLEU results on WMT '14 EN-DE (Table 1) and GLUE results for BERT + POS (Table 2), and analyzes attention visualizations to justify syntactic effects. These statements and quantitative results in the full text directly satisfy both required Stage 2 inclusion criteria. No Stage 2 exclusion criteria are clearly triggered by the provided text.

title: Syntactic Control of Language Models by Posterior Inference

evidence_highlights: Abstract: "Our experiments with GPT2 and Llama3-8B models show that with an appropriate proposal distribution, we can improve syntactic accuracy, increasing the F1 score from 12.31 (GPT2-large) and 35.33 (Llama3-8B) to about 93 in both cases without compromising the language model’s fluency."

Full text: "We then apply our method to GPT2-large (Radford et al., 2019), as well as to Llama3-8B models (Llama Team, 2024). Sentences generated using our method adhere much more closely to the target syntax than those produced without it. For instance, with a well-chosen proposal distribution, our method improves the F1 score of GPT2-large generations from 12.31 to 93.69 without degrading the model’s fluency."
Full text (method): "We train Tetratagger using the GPT2 and Llama3-8B models as the language model backbone. We later use these taggers to control the decoding process."
review_output.decision_rationale: 全文明確以 Transformer-based LMs（GPT2、Llama3-8B、GPT4）為對象，提出並實作基於 sequential Monte Carlo 與 tetratagger 的語法控制方法，並以定量實驗（bracketing F1、−log φ/−log p 等）展示大幅提升（例：F1 從 ~12 提升到 ~93）。符合兩項必須納入條件，且無觸發任何排除條件，故給予強納入。
